beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, and morning routine at home in Madrona.
beats.reasoning: Early riser habit; Felix wakes before 7am most days. Checks weather (91F, clear, smoke haze), mentally notes the heat and decides to plan outdoor time carefully and hydrate well.

beats.activity: Drive from Madrona to First Hill, tax season work day begins.
beats.reasoning: Tax season is ongoing; Felix commutes to the office. Early departure avoids heat buildup in car and morning traffic.

beats.activity: Client meetings, reconciliations, and spreadsheet work at the office.
beats.reasoning: Tax season rhythm: morning block of focused work. Air quality is moderate (smoke), so staying indoors at the office is practical.

beats.activity: Lunch at a favorite food truck on Cherry Street (in shade), hydrate well given the heat.
beats.reasoning: Felix knows every food truck rotation; he's grabbed lunch here before. On a 91F day, staying under the awning or shade is smart. Hydration is key with smoke and heat.

beats.activity: Afternoon work: follow-ups, more spreadsheets, client calls.
beats.reasoning: Tax season afternoon grind. Felix stays at the office through the peak heat hours (2–4pm); indoors is safer with the smoke and heat.

beats.activity: Drive from First Hill to Madrona, heading home.
beats.reasoning: Leaves office around 5:45pm to beat the worst of the evening heat and smoke buildup. Arrives home in time to freshen up before dinner.

beats.activity: Shower, change, and light dinner at home—hydrating meal with plenty of water.
beats.reasoning: Post-work wind-down. Heat and smoke mean Felix prioritizes hydration and a cool indoor space before the evening.

beats.activity: Walk/short drive from Madrona to Gas Works Park for the outdoor concert.
beats.reasoning: The free 6pm concert at Gas Works draws crowds; Felix enjoys community events and live music. Evening temps are cooling, and the park offers open air despite smoke. Close friends likely to be there.

beats.activity: Enjoy outdoor concert at Gas Works Park, social time, light refreshments.
beats.reasoning: Felix values time with friends over big crowds, but this is a smaller community concert (not a festival). The evening cool and open air make it pleasant despite the smoke haze. He can leave if it feels too crowded.
Concert winds down around 9:15pm. Felix heads home to settle in before bed.

diary: Started the day early in Madrona with my usual ritual—coffee while the light was still soft, before the heat moved in. The drive to First Hill felt longer than usual; maybe I was already thinking about the numbers waiting for me. Tax season doesn't let up, even in June. Spent the morning in client meetings, then grabbed lunch at that food truck on Cherry Street. Smart move sitting in the shade—by afternoon it was brutal, pushing 91 degrees. The office felt stuffy despite the AC, so I drank water like it was going out of style. After work, I showered off the day and had something light at home. But I wasn't ready to call it a night. The concert at Gas Works was calling, and I needed to shake off the spreadsheets. The park was packed, but there's something about live music under a clear sky that reminds you why you do the desk work in the first place. Felt alive again.
thought_process: I knew today would be a grind—tax season always is—but I made deliberate choices to manage it. The heat was a factor; I pushed hydration hard after noticing the air quality warning from those BC fires. Lunch in shade wasn't lazy, it was tactical. By afternoon I was tired but not destroyed, which meant I had enough left to enjoy the concert. The real decision was going at all. Part of me wanted to collapse on the couch, but I remembered why I hike and why I love being outdoors. The concert felt like permission to be human again, not just an accountant. The casual social time, the music, the crowd energy—it balanced the solitary focus of the workday. Some days you need to earn the evening.
world_event_prompt: Hot, clear Wednesday hitting 91F by mid-afternoon. Free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park from 6pm draws big crowds. Moderate air quality from distant BC wildfire smoke.
